title: The One With The Cake

jokes.text: Gee, if only she were 1 and had no idea what the hell a birthday was.
jokes.setup: Ross insisting they can't leave because it's Emma's real birthday
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious absurdity that a 1-year-old has no concept of birthdays
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with perfect timing. The 'if only' construction is elegantly structured, and it cuts right to the heart of the parental delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We can't leave! It's Emma's real birthday!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ross, she's one. She has no idea what a birthday is. She can't even say 'birthday.' She just thinks people are yelling at her.

jokes.text: As much as I hate to delay your doing weird sex stuff to my little sister.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Monica wanting to leave for their romantic weekend
jokes.punchline: Ross acknowledging their sexual relationship in the most uncomfortable way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ross being simultaneously supportive and deeply uncomfortable about his sister's sex life.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Have a great time, you two. And hey, try not to... you know... do too much of the... thing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The thing?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, the... horizontal... the... uh... the thing that married people do.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross!

jokes.text: I promise I won't do any magic.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to convince everyone to stay for the party
jokes.punchline: Offering to not do magic as if that's a major selling point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character beat that reveals Ross thinks his magic is both impressive and potentially annoying enough to be used as negotiation leverage.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Come on, guys, please stay for the party! I promise I won't do any magic tricks.

jokes.text: Believe me, that is not why we won't be doing that.
jokes.setup: Chandler worried they won't do the Maxim thing because of the party
jokes.punchline: Monica implying there are other reasons they won't be doing it
jokes.explanation: Solid marital humor with good timing, though fairly standard relationship comedy territory.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: They're not going to do the Maxim thing because of the party.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, there are other reasons we won't be doing it.

jokes.text: When Ursula and I were kids, on our birthday... our stepdad would sell his blood to buy us food.
jokes.setup: Sweet story about Ross's dad hiding birthday presents and drawing treasure maps
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's characteristically dark childhood memory presented as equivalent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - the stark contrast between Ross's wholesome memory and her tragic one, delivered with her characteristic obliviousness to how dark it is.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My father used to hide things too. Course, he was usually hiding from the police, but... it's sweet that you have that memory.

jokes.text: And they'll be living in a floating city that the humans built... to escape the ant people.
jokes.setup: Normal speculation about Emma watching the video in the future on new technology
jokes.punchline: Escalating to completely absurd sci-fi scenario about ant people
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdism - starts with plausible future speculation and careens into complete nonsense with perfect confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what if in the future they have some kind of technology where Emma can watch this video on a little screen in her eye?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, Phoebe...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, listen! And then they can also put a little speaker in her ear so she can hear it, and then one day she's just walking down the street and it just starts playing!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that would be so weird.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Or—or what if by then we're all living underground because the sun exploded, and we're all tiny because food is scarce, and we have to communicate through a series of clicks and whistles!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Phoebe, I think the sun is going to be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Or what if we're all just brains in jars, and the only thing that exists is this video, playing over and over again!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why would they do that?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't know! Or—or what if intelligent ant people take over the world and they decide to study human history, and this is the only record we have left, and they show it to their ant children and say, 'This is what humans were like'?

jokes.text: That's the hope.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's insane ant people scenario
jokes.punchline: Ross treating it as a reasonable aspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response with incredible economy. Ross's deadpan acceptance makes Phoebe's absurdity even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And then one day, the Queen ant will choose me, and I'll become the Princess of the ants.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, that's... that's a very specific goal. Have you thought about what you'd do as Princess?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'd make sure all the ants are happy and well-fed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right, right. No, that's good. That's a solid five-year plan.

jokes.text: I said, it's still naptime.
jokes.setup: Monica asking where Joey is
jokes.punchline: Implying Joey is also napping like a child
jokes.explanation: Solid character joke playing on Joey's childlike nature, well-constructed but not groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Where's Joey?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He's napping.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Napping? It's the middle of the day!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he said he was tired.

jokes.text: What? What? It's for her hot friends.
jokes.setup: Joey being told not to say something inappropriate to 18-year-old Emma
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's thinking about Emma's future friends sexually
jokes.explanation: Very Joey moment but relies on his established horndog character trait. Mildly uncomfortable rather than genuinely funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm not gonna say anything to her. I'm just gonna be thinking about her friends when they get older.

jokes.text: When they see this, you'll be 52. And starting to think about settling down.
jokes.setup: Joey planning to hit on Emma's future friends
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he'll be middle-aged and still acting like a bachelor
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Joey's arrested development. The 'starting to think about settling down' is a perfect character-specific detail.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: When Emma's older, I'm gonna meet all her friends.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, you'll be in your fifties!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So, you can't hit on your best friend's daughter's friends when you're in your fifties!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because it won't be cute anymore, it'll be a federal offense.

jokes.text: Yeah, but for a 1-year-old. What's the point? The other day she laughed for an hour at a cup.
jokes.setup: Joey questioning why you'd bring presents to a 1-year-old's party
jokes.punchline: Citing Emma's easily amused nature as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good observational setup for the extended cup joke. Joey's practical confusion is well-characterized.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why would you bring presents to a one-year-old's birthday party? They don't even know what's going on.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because it's a birthday party, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but she's one. She's easily amused. I mean, look at her — she's been staring at that ribbon for twenty minutes.

jokes.text: Just a cup with a picture of Elmo on it, dressed as a farmer. And he's standing next to this cow, and the cow says, 'El-moo.'
jokes.setup: Joey explaining what made Emma laugh
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun on the cup that's actually pretty funny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure - Joey dismisses the cup as simple, then describes it in detail, revealing it's actually quite clever. The pun is genuinely funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She laughed at a pun on my coffee cup.

jokes.text: Man, that's a funny cup.
jokes.setup: Joey's detailed description of the Elmo cup pun
jokes.punchline: His sincere appreciation for the humor he just dismissed
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the cup sequence. Joey's genuine appreciation undercuts his original dismissal beautifully.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait... that's actually genius. 'Elmo cup'? Like 'L-M-O'? That's hilarious!

jokes.text: She was probably up all night excited about the party she knows is happening.
jokes.setup: Emma still being asleep during her own birthday party
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggesting a 1-year-old was anticipating her party
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcasm but fairly obvious given the setup. Not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm sure she was really looking forward to this. I bet she spent all week practicing her surprise face.

jokes.text: Are you frigging kidding me, Greene?
jokes.setup: Being told they can't wake Emma up after being convinced to stay
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated reaction using Ross's last name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's frustration. Using the last name adds formality that makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We can't wake her up? We can't wake her up?! Geller, she's a baby, not a sleeping pill!

jokes.text: Ross was jealous of all the attention we were giving you. He pulled on his testicles so hard... we had to take him to the emergency room.
jokes.setup: Judy reminiscing about Monica's first birthday
jokes.punchline: Revealing Ross's bizarre attention-seeking behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable family revelation. The specificity and extremeness make it both shocking and hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: Monica's first birthday was so special. We had the most wonderful party planned.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: But then Ross decided he needed to be the center of attention. He put on his father's glasses and started doing this ridiculous dance.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: Everyone just stared at him. Nobody knew what he was doing. We never did figure it out.

jokes.text: There's something you didn't know about your dad.
jokes.setup: Just revealed Ross's testicle-pulling incident
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that highlights the absurdity of what was just revealed through understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: A testicle? Ross, that's not a testicle, that's a...

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: ...a little bit of a problem.

jokes.text: Which one are you?
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting they give Emma a present from all three of them
jokes.punchline: Jack not recognizing Joey despite knowing him for years
jokes.explanation: Devastating in its brevity. Jack's casual confusion about Joey's identity is perfectly in character and brutally funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We should get her a present from all three of us.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: Who is this?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's me, Joey! We've known each other for years!

jokes.text: I'm going to ram this platter really hard into your ribs. You're gonna scream out, and that'll wake her up.
jokes.setup: Monica frustrated that Emma is still asleep
jokes.punchline: Her completely unhinged plan involving physical violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her controlling nature pushed to violent extremes. The casual delivery of the insane plan is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Emma's still asleep? It's already 9 AM! I've been up since 6, I've cleaned the apartment twice, reorganized the pantry... Okay, you know what? Here's what we're gonna do. We're gonna go in there, we're gonna grab that pillow, and we're gonna hit her with it until she wakes up. And if that doesn't work, we'll try the other pillow. And if THAT doesn't work, we move on to the couch cushions!

jokes.text: I'm not going to Vermont with this Monica.
jokes.setup: Monica's violent platter plan
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating Monica like she has multiple personalities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reaction that acknowledges Monica's extreme behavior shift. 'This Monica' suggests there are different versions.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, Monica, we need to talk about this platter situation. I'm sensing some... hostility.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: There is no hostility! I'm fine!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, and how is... the other Monica feeling?

jokes.text: So you're gonna hit on her?
jokes.setup: Joey saying he wants to use his talents for Emma
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately assuming it's his talent for hitting on women
jokes.explanation: Perfect character knowledge - Phoebe knows Joey so well she immediately identifies his primary 'talent.' Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I want to use my talents for Emma.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, you're gonna teach her how to hit on women?

jokes.text: Or you could stick a fork in an apple.
jokes.setup: Joey wanting to do a dramatic reading
jokes.punchline: Monica's dismissive alternative suggestion
jokes.explanation: Solid putdown with good economy. Monica's practicality cutting through Joey's pretension.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I want to do a dramatic reading of something really moving.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, that's great, Joey! You could read the phone book.

jokes.text: I do it every week with three cameras pointed at me and a whole crew waiting.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he'll be embarrassed having nothing prepared
jokes.punchline: Joey referencing his soap opera work
jokes.explanation: Mild meta humor and good character moment showing Joey's professional confidence, but not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Won't you be embarrassed having nothing prepared?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, I'm an actor. I've done soap operas. I can do anything on the spot.

jokes.text: Riding the Storm Out: Coping With Postpartum Depression.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he'll read one of Emma's favorite books
jokes.punchline: Revealing he grabbed an adult self-help book instead of a children's book
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. The title is so wrong for a baby's birthday that it's shocking and hilarious. Perfect Joey mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, I got Emma's favorite book right here.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Men Are from Mars, Women Are from Venus.

jokes.text: Love You Forever, by Robert Munsch. Published by Firefly Books. Printed... in Mexico.
jokes.setup: Joey doing a dramatic reading of a children's book
jokes.punchline: His overly theatrical delivery including publication details
jokes.explanation: Joey's actor training making him read literally everything with dramatic emphasis. The Mexico detail is particularly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The Velveteen Rabbit, by Margery Williams. Illustrated by William Nicholson. Published by Doubleday, Page and Company, Garden City, New York, 1922.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Once there was a little boy who owned a toy rabbit...

jokes.text: I was not ready for this today.
jokes.setup: Joey's emotional reading of the children's book
jokes.punchline: Ross being genuinely moved by the performance
jokes.explanation: Good emotional beat that shows even Joey's amateur dramatics can be affecting. Ross's vulnerability is well-played.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The duckling said to his mother, 'I will always love you.'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: *wiping tears* That was... that was beautiful, Joe.

jokes.text: Maybe the actor Richard Crenna. He played the commanding officer in Rambo.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to find words that rhyme with Emma
jokes.punchline: Her bizarrely specific reference to Richard Crenna's military role
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Phoebe knowledge. The specificity of the Rambo reference makes it funnier than just saying 'Richard Crenna.'

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so I need a word that rhymes with Emma. Uh... lemma! Hemma? Stemma?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! I got it! It's like when Richard Crenna was a colonel in Rambo!

jokes.text: Sex and the Single Mother... Finding your G-spot.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she prepared a reading too
jokes.punchline: The completely inappropriate book titles for a baby's party
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and very Phoebe to not understand why these would be inappropriate. The double reveal works well.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! I prepared a reading too. It's from my book, 'Oh, The Places You'll Go... If You Don't Die First: A Realistic Look at the World for Infants.'

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And Chapter Three: 'The Asbestos in Your Crib.'

jokes.text: This message could be coming to you... from beyond the grave, Emma... After all, my parents died very young.
jokes.setup: Recording a message for Emma's 18th birthday
jokes.punchline: Immediately turning morbid about their own mortality
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate for a birthday message. The Gellers' tendency toward morbidity is perfectly captured.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: Hi Emma, it's Dad. Happy 18th birthday, sweetheart.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: We're so proud of you, honey. You've grown into such a wonderful young woman.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: Of course, by the time you watch this, your mother and I will probably be dead.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: Very dead, Emma. Like, decomposed.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: We probably won't even have caskets anymore—just dust in a jar.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: If you're lucky, maybe you'll recognize a tooth.

jokes.text: Remember, Emma, heart disease kills women too.
jokes.setup: Already discussing mortality in Emma's birthday message
jokes.punchline: Adding specific medical warnings
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the morbid birthday message. Judy's matter-of-fact delivery of health warnings is hilariously inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: And make sure to tell her about the side effects. Nausea, dizziness, and in rare cases, spontaneous combustion.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom, that's not a real side effect.

dialogue.speaker: Judy Geller
dialogue.line: Well, it could be. You never know with these things.

jokes.text: I used to hitchhike there when I was a kid.
jokes.setup: Talking about how great the bakery's frosting is
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning dangerous childhood behavior for cake
jokes.explanation: Great character detail showing Monica's food obsession from an early age, delivered with perfect casualness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, the frosting is amazing. You know, when I was a kid, I used to eat frosting right out of the container.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My mom would chase me around the kitchen with a spatula.

jokes.text: They put my baby's face on a penis!
jokes.setup: Ross discovering the cake mix-up
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified realization about the cake shape
jokes.explanation: Peak sitcom moment. The sentence is inherently absurd and shocking. Rachel's maternal horror makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, why is there a giant penis cake in our kitchen?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God! That's not supposed to be here! That's for my bachelorette party!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What happened to the actual wedding cake?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh no. Oh no no no. I think... I think that went to the guy's birthday party.

jokes.text: Now it's a party!
jokes.setup: Discovery of the penis cake
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic response to the inappropriate cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction - where everyone else is horrified, he's delighted. Great economy and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! What's not to like? Chocolate, vanilla, it's not weird!

jokes.text: Is it okay that I still think it looks delicious?
jokes.setup: Everyone's horror at the penis cake
jokes.punchline: Monica's food obsession overriding her disgust
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her love of good food overcoming the awkwardness. The hesitant delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, that's disgusting, that's—wait, is that fondant? Because fondant is really hard to work with, and whoever did this actually did a pretty good job with the shading.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? This is actually kind of impressive.

jokes.text: The resemblance is uncanny.
jokes.setup: Looking at Emma's face on the penis cake
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact comment about the resemblance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly deadpan delivery that makes the awkward situation even more uncomfortable. Jack's obliviousness is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: It looks like Emma.

jokes.text: I am this close to tugging on my testicles again.
jokes.setup: The overwhelming awkwardness of the penis cake situation
jokes.punchline: Ross referencing his childhood attention-seeking behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Ross is still prone to the same attention-seeking behavior. The callback timing is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I used to do things like this as a kid to get attention.

jokes.text: Ironically, that was the Maxim thing.
jokes.setup: Ross threatening to tug on his testicles
jokes.punchline: Revealing that was Chandler and Monica's planned activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that ties together two running gags brilliantly. The word 'ironically' makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: If you don't stop, I'm gonna tug on your testicles!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! That's what Monica and I had planned for tonight!

jokes.text: Ask them if it would be faster if we cut the baby's face off the penis... so we could put it on the bunny. That is a weird sentence.
jokes.setup: Trying to fix the cake situation quickly
jokes.punchline: Ross acknowledging how bizarre the sentence sounds
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where the character acknowledges the absurdity. The practical solution makes the situation even weirder.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We need to fix this cake situation fast!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just said 'I'm gonna go put on some pants' and that sounded completely normal.

jokes.text: To be fair, this one does have nuts.
jokes.setup: Rachel specifying she wants nuts on her cake
jokes.punchline: Chandler's double entendre about the penis cake
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works on multiple levels. Chandler's timing and delivery would make this land well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I want nuts on my cake.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I want a penis on mine.

jokes.text: It was nice of her to pull my hair... till I dropped the key.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying it was nice of Monica to loan Rachel her car
jokes.punchline: Revealing Rachel had to physically force Monica to give up the keys
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's controlling nature and Rachel's desperation. The casual violence is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That was nice of you to loan Rachel your car.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't really have a choice. She tackled me in the parking garage.

jokes.text: Are you still enjoying your nap?
jokes.setup: Recording a message for Emma in 2020
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically referencing Emma's current nap that's ruining the party
jokes.explanation: Good callback that bridges the current frustration with the future message format. Monica's passive-aggression works.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hey Emma, it's Aunt Monica. We're all here celebrating, and you're napping? Really? You're ruining the party!

jokes.text: We used to be married. But then we missed a weekend away together, and things kind of unraveled... because of you.
jokes.setup: Recording a message for adult Emma
jokes.punchline: Blaming their future divorce on missing this weekend
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation of their current frustration. The matter-of-fact delivery of relationship destruction is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey Emma, it's your dad. Your mom and I just wanted to say how sorry we are that we're missing your birthday this weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, we feel terrible about it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But we want you to know that this weekend is really important to us, and we'll make it up to you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Absolutely. We love you so much.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And when your mother and I get divorced, we want you to know it's not because we don't love each other...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's because of this weekend.

jokes.text: I can't believe I taped over my Big Brother audition for this.
jokes.setup: Another failed video message for Emma
jokes.punchline: Revealing Chandler sacrificed something important for this disaster
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's frustration. The Big Brother detail adds specificity but isn't exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm ready. Hi Emma, it's Uncle Chandler. I just want to say that you are the most beautiful baby in the whole world, and I love you very much.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That was great, Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? Because I gave up my whole afternoon for this. I had to cancel my erectile dysfunction support group.

jokes.text: You'll tell us on Monday.
jokes.setup: Joey asking how Rachel will feel when she comes back and everyone's gone
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact acceptance that they're leaving
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that shows Chandler has completely given up on the party. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How do you think Rachel's gonna feel when she comes back and finds out we're all gone?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Devastated. But she'll get over it.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Get the hell out of here!
jokes.setup: Joey supposedly guarding everyone to prevent them from leaving
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately telling someone to leave when caught
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his complete failure at the guard duty. Character-consistent but not remarkable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Get out! Get out of here!

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just go! Now!

jokes.text: Last winter I went up on a church lawn and drove right through a manger scene. The papers thought it was a hate crime.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Jack can't drive at night
jokes.punchline: His accidentally blasphemous driving incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple driving problems to accidental religious vandalism. The 'hate crime' detail is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Dad can't drive at night anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He almost hit a nun.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A nun?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, and he said, 'I didn't even see her until she flew across the hood!'

jokes.text: No, he didn't. He pretended to be a Quaker to get out of Korea.
jokes.setup: Joey saying Jack fought for the country
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jack was actually a draft dodger
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that undercuts Joey's hero worship. The Quaker detail adds specificity and humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Jack fought for the country.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, Jack was a draft dodger.

jokes.text: You do know I can just turn them the other way around, right? Oh, I forgot you used to live here.
jokes.setup: Joey locking everyone in by turning the door locks
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out the obvious flaw, Joey remembering Chandler's knowledge
jokes.explanation: Solid logic joke showing Joey's plan failing immediately. The callback to living arrangements works.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you locked us in. How are we supposed to get out?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We can't. I have the only key.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, the door opens from the inside. There's a handle.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh. Right. Chandler knows about doors.

jokes.text: I got it.
jokes.setup: Joey realizing he doesn't have a prepared monologue for his audition
jokes.punchline: His sudden realization about using his book reading
jokes.explanation: Simple callback moment. Joey's problem-solving is in character but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh no, oh no, oh no! I don't have a monologue prepared!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait... I could use that book I've been reading!

jokes.text: Okay, 10. Okay, Monica picks 10. I call nine.
jokes.setup: Trying to decide who leaves first by picking numbers 1-10
jokes.punchline: Monica and Chandler immediately picking the highest numbers
jokes.explanation: Shows their desperation to leave but is fairly predictable. Character-appropriate but not inspired.

jokes.text: That's a bear! I'm too excited!
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she wants the dolphin toy
jokes.punchline: Her mistake and enthusiastic explanation
jokes.explanation: Cute Phoebe moment showing her excitement overriding her perception. In character but minor.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want the dolphin toy!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh wait, I thought you said you wanted to do something with a *fin*. Like, I was gonna say, I'm not really into that, but I respect your choices.

jokes.text: I bought you! How did I forget that that's all you do?
jokes.setup: Monica's wind-up dog just barking instead of racing
jokes.punchline: Her frustrated realization about the toy's limitation
jokes.explanation: Good Monica moment - her need to win being thwarted by a defective toy. The personal betrayal is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's supposed to race around the room, not just stand there barking!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I paid twenty dollars for a dog that doesn't even run. It just... barks.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? This is exactly like my last relationship.

jokes.text: They left.
jokes.setup: Monica arguing about the race rules while others escape
jokes.punchline: Her belated realization everyone abandoned her argument
jokes.explanation: Good deflation moment. Monica's obsession with rules while missing the bigger picture is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! You can't just run backwards, that's not how racing works! The rules clearly state—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hello? Guys? Where did everyone go?

jokes.text: You're such a good driver.
jokes.setup: Rachel getting a speeding ticket
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic compliment
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm, not particularly clever or memorable. Functional but uninspired.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wow, a speeding ticket. That's so cool. You know what? You should get more of those.

jokes.text: Hey, get out of the road, you stupid student driver! They have to learn.
jokes.setup: Rachel yelling at a student driver
jokes.punchline: Ross's patient response contrasting with her road rage
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Ross being more reasonable than Rachel for once. Minor but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, MOVE IT! What are you doing?! You're going like five miles an hour! There are people behind you!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, he's a student driver. He's probably very nervous.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't care! Learn to drive or stay home!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, when I was teaching Ben to drive, I appreciated it when people were patient with him.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, that's very sweet, but this guy is testing my patience!

jokes.text: Well, I just made these two things cheeks. And then I split this to make ears.
jokes.setup: Ross successfully turning the penis cake into a bunny
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact explanation of the anatomical transformation
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback with Ross's casual explanation of his cake surgery. The transformation is genuinely clever.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, you see, if you just remove the shaft, the base and the two balls form the bunny's head and ears.

jokes.text: I, apparently, can turn phallic cakes into woodland creatures.
jokes.setup: Ross's successful cake transformation
jokes.punchline: His discovery of his oddly specific talent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that perfectly captures the absurdity of the situation. The formal phrasing makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just realized I have an oddly specific talent.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What, you can turn cakes into... other cakes?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I can identify cakes by their molecular structure.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's the least useful superpower ever.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, it's saved me from eating a sponge twice!

jokes.text: Did I teach her that? Did I just impart wisdom?
jokes.setup: Emma seemingly responding to Ross's question about her age
jokes.punchline: Ross's proud overreaction to basic interaction
jokes.explanation: Good parental pride moment. Ross's excitement over minimal interaction is very dad-like.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: How old are you now, Emma?

dialogue.speaker: Emma
dialogue.line: Five.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Five! That's incredible! Did you hear that, everyone? She can count!

jokes.text: There's no one around. Why don't we just take this one? And head to Canada.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler wanting a baby
jokes.punchline: Escalating from wanting one to kidnapping and fleeing the country
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their baby fever. Canada as the escape destination adds specificity and humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We could adopt.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Or we could steal one.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm kidding. We'd never get caught anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, we're fleeing to Canada.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll get the passports.

jokes.text: I just felt so bad missing this. So I just slipped him a little something, you know.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she's back from her massage client
jokes.punchline: Implying she drugged her client
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe to casually mention drugging someone. Her matter-of-fact delivery of something shocking works perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I had to leave early. My client fell asleep.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I think I used too much of the lavender oil... or not enough.

jokes.text: Joey, we just saw you come in. You ran past us on the stairs.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he's been there the whole time
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out they literally just watched him arrive
jokes.explanation: Standard calling-out-the-obvious humor. Joey's terrible lying is in character but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We literally just watched you arrive.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No you didn't.

jokes.text: No! No science camp!
jokes.setup: Ross excited about Emma's apparent intelligence
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate fear of Ross's academic influence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Rachel's fear of Ross's nerdy influence on Emma. The panic is well-played.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, she can do that already? She's only six months old!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, right? She's incredibly smart. Just like her old man.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh God, she's going to end up like you, isn't she? All nerdy and talking about dinosaurs at parties.

jokes.text: What is wrong with me? It looked more delicious when it was a penis.
jokes.setup: Monica looking at the transformed bunny cake
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission about preferring the penis version
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her food obsession overriding social appropriateness. The self-awareness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I have to say, I preferred it when it looked like a penis.

jokes.text: Go! Go, Alan! Run, you hairy bastard!
jokes.setup: Joey's video being recorded over the birthday message
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic commentary on the toy race
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Joey's continued investment in the race. The insult to the toy is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! And he's coming around the turn! Look at the little guy go! He's really picking up speed now!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's gonna make it! He's gonna make it! Yes! YES!

tmdb_overview: On Emma's first birthday, Ross and Rachel convince everyone to delay their plans so they can attend her party, however plans go awry when Emma's birthday cake is revealed.
insight: Sustained character comedy and escalation hit 84.8 with nearly three jokes per minute across the finale run.
